name: Energy Sector (Global)
summary: Day 80 (Monday). **ENERGY XLE +2.4% MONDAY ($59.44) ON BRENT $111.47 (+2.0%) — STANCHART $130-145 PATH ACTIVATES.** Exxon Mobil +4.1% to $157.92; Energy sector breadth strongest of cycle. Crisis-linked ~95%. **Driver chain**: (i) UAE Barakah perimeter strike + Saudi drone intercept + Trump-Netanyahu coordination + Trump 'clock is ticking' weekend; (ii) 2-session sustain rule fires Brent trigger (StanChart $130-145 cascade); (iii) Aramco CEO Nasser line ~30 vessels/day vs 138 pre-war Hormuz; (iv) VLCC TD3C Baltic Exchange Week 18 partial WS408.13 (~9x pre-war). **Reversibility**: Tuesday May 19 NSC strike order → Brent $120-130 → Exxon $165-170, XLE $62-64; back-off → Brent retraces $5-8 → Exxon $152-155, XLE $57-58; nothing operational → Brent oscillates $107-112, XLE holds $58-60. **Lockheed -15.1% 1M / Cheniere -7.7% 1M / Frontline -1.1% 1D** = defense/LNG/tanker complex NOT bidding even on Monday's Brent gap = paper-physical bifurcation between energy producers (bid) and energy infrastructure (not bid) at cycle extreme. **PRIOR (May 7): Day 69 Thursday. PEACE-DIVIDEND ROTATION DESPITE FLAT SPOT OIL — XLE -4.1% TO $57.00; EXXON -4.0% TO $148.69; CHENIERE LNG -3.0% TO $261.42; RBOB GASOLINE -3.7%.** This is the cleanest 'war beneficiary unwind' signal in the tape today. Forward-pricing of Brent $86-90 (Goldman Q2 $90 + World Bank 2026 average $86) is doing the work — spot Brent is flat today but equity is pricing normalization. Reuters/Investing.com explicit attribution May 7: 'U.S. energy sector stocks falling amid optimism that Middle East diplomacy will yield a peace agreement.' XLE is down -5.3% 1M now. Exxon -4.0% reflects YTD peak unwind + Q1 earnings residual (already reported May 1). **Cheniere -3.0% specific:** LNG-scarcity premium (Qatar Ras Laffan damage thesis) being partially repriced as European TTF gas crashed -6.8% May 6 to ~€47.4/MWh; Cheniere reported Q1 today with modest miss on guidance forward — adds to peace-dividend rotation. **Reversibility:** if MOU signs, XLE down another 5-10% on full peace pricing; if Iran rejects, XLE bounces +6-10% within 48hr, Exxon back toward $156-160, Cheniere toward $275-280. URA $58.26 (+7.5%) is the rare asset benefiting from BOTH peace track (HEU custodian path under MOU) and structural AI power demand (Cameco 24% of URA, $2.7B 10-year US enrichment commitment continues).
